title: Connection Reuse, Pools, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and Concurrency · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Concurrency Saturation
summary: A daemon sends concurrent requests through HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 pools while idle connections, bodies, and stream limits affect capacity. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go concurrency saturation increment in a Go 1.26 net/http connection lifecycle and concurrency plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound concurrent requests, queued work, connections, streams, memory, and downstream pressure from measured capacity. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Explain HTTP/3 over QUIC, independent streams, connection migration, negotiation, fallback, and observability limits. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Concurrency Saturation · Theory Concurrency Saturation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model schedules 40 operations across HTTP/1.1 keep-alive, an HTTP/2 connection limited to 5 streams, and optional HTTP/3. Bound queues and memory, reuse clients, finish bodies, and distinguish transport from semantics.
